title: AlexNet wins ImageNet
description: A deep convolutional neural network dramatically outperforms prior approaches on the ImageNet competition, kicking off the modern deep learning boom.

title: "Attention Is All You Need" introduces the Transformer
description: Google researchers publish the Transformer architecture, which becomes the foundation for nearly every major language model that follows.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-1
description: The first Generative Pre-trained Transformer paper demonstrates that unsupervised pretraining followed by fine-tuning works well for language tasks.

title: OpenAI announces GPT-2
description: A significantly larger language model that OpenAI initially withheld full release of, citing misuse concerns — an early flashpoint in AI safety debates.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-3
description: A 175-billion-parameter model that popularizes few-shot prompting and shows dramatic gains from scale, reshaping expectations for what language models can do.

title: OpenAI announces DALL-E
description: One of the first widely-noticed text-to-image models, showing the same transformer approach generalizing well beyond text.

title: DeepMind publishes the Chinchilla scaling laws
description: Research showing many large models of the era were undertrained relative to their size, reshaping how labs balance model size against training data volume.

title: ChatGPT launches
description: OpenAI's chat interface over GPT-3.5 becomes the fastest-growing consumer application in history, bringing large language models to a mainstream audience overnight.

title: Meta releases LLaMA
description: Meta's first LLaMA model family, initially released to researchers, becomes the foundation of a large open-weight ecosystem after weights leak publicly.

title: GPT-4 and Claude both arrive
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4 and Anthropic releases the first Claude model in the same month, marking the start of sustained multi-lab frontier competition.

title: Anthropic releases Claude 2
description: A significant capability and context-window jump for Anthropic's model line, expanding usable context to 100K tokens.

title: Google announces Gemini
description: Google's Gemini family launches as a natively multimodal model built from the ground up rather than bolting vision onto a text model.

title: Anthropic releases the Claude 3 family
description: Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku launch as a tiered family trading off capability for cost and speed — a pattern other labs converge on.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-4o
description: A faster, natively multimodal model with real-time voice conversation, offered free to a much wider tier of users.

title: OpenAI releases o1
description: The first widely-used "reasoning model" that spends extra inference-time compute working through a problem before answering, starting an industry-wide reasoning-model era.

title: DeepSeek releases R1
description: An open-weight reasoning model matching much of the frontier's capability at a fraction of the training and inference cost, disrupting assumptions about compute moats.

title: Anthropic releases Claude 3.7 Sonnet
description: A hybrid model that can answer quickly or switch into an extended step-by-step reasoning mode within the same model.

title: Meta releases the Llama 4 family
description: Scout and Maverick launch with very large context windows and a mixture-of-experts architecture, continuing Meta's open-weight strategy.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-5
description: A unified model that internally routes between fast and deep-reasoning modes, replacing the separate GPT-4-series and o-series naming.

title: Prediction: agentic AI becomes the default interface for complex software work
description: Industry watchers expect autonomous, multi-step agentic tool use — rather than single-turn chat — to become the primary way developers and knowledge workers interact with frontier models.

title: Prediction: on-device frontier-class models reach flagship phones
description: Continued efficiency gains are expected to bring near-frontier model capability to on-device inference on high-end consumer hardware, reducing reliance on cloud API calls for everyday tasks.

title: Prediction: AI-assisted scientific discovery reaches real-world deployment
description: Several labs are racing to claim the first widely recognized case of an AI system materially contributing to a deployed scientific discovery, such as a novel material or drug candidate.

title: Prediction: frontier AI regulation converges across major economies
description: Analysts anticipate the patchwork of national AI regulations (US, EU, China, and others) will move toward greater — though incomplete — alignment on safety testing and disclosure requirements for frontier models.
